signals.headline: Japan Ditching SWIFT for XRP-Powered Remittances Confirmed | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Japan Ditching SWIFT for XRP-Powered Remittances Confirmed | YES | MED confidence**  
Fresh reports today highlight Japan adopting XRP for remittances, replacing SWIFT infrastructure, which could drive massive enterprise adoption and volume amid April's price surge past $1.50.[4][7]

signals.headline: SEC Lawsuit Resolution Fuels XRP's 10% April Surge to $1
signals.raw:
SEC Lawsuit Resolution Fuels XRP's 10% April Surge to $1.42 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The SEC's decision to drop its case against Ripple Labs—circulating as confirmed news this month—has removed a multi-year overhang, enabling technical breakouts and bullish analyst targets up to $5 by year-end.[2]

signals.headline: Ripple Reveals Four-Phase Plan to Make XRP Ledger Quantum-Safe by 2028 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ripple Reveals Four-Phase Plan to Make XRP Ledger Quantum-Safe by 2028 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This breaking announcement, reported just hours ago on April 22, 2026, details proactive post-quantum cryptography testing starting H2 2026 on Devnet, positioning XRPL as future-proof against quantum threats and signaling long-term institutional appeal.[3][7]

signals.raw: **Event Details**: On Sunday (April 5, 2026), Dallas Mavericks rookie **Cooper Flagg** scored 45 points (14-for-27 FG), with 9 assists and 8 rebounds, securing a 134-128 victory over the **Los Angeles Lakers**; this followed his career-high 51 points on Friday vs. Orlando, marking back-to-back 40+ point games—the first for a rookie since Allen Iverson—and surpassing LeBron James for most 40-point
